title: THE RESCUE — ECHO DIRECTOR'S CUT V9
episodeObjective: Guide Maya through the unstable rescue path, expose Hale's old pod access, force a permanent open/abort decision, and carry visible physical, psychological, relationship, and signal consequences into Episode 10.

summary: Core spine preserved: rescue junction, beacon stabilization/rush, route choice, pod exterior, Hale old access, access protocol, open/abort branch.
Every visible choice set has exactly two choices.
CharacterBusy durations reduced to 35–60 seconds for real-time pacing.
Report-style action lines were rewritten as lived physical moments.
Maya and Hale visibly remember operator choices inside the same episode.
Route A creates radiation/suit consequences; Route B creates pod-time and guilt consequences.
Secure/inspect branch now affects pod stability and Maya's shoulder state.
Old-log/focus branch now affects Hale trust and Maya resentment.
Vent/force branch now creates distinct Episode 10 pod states.
Open/abort final branch creates irreversible Episode 10 start states.
UNKNOWN messages now push story and pressure decisions instead of generic glitch noise.
Final branches preserve Noah ambiguity while increasing consequence weight.

e09_n01.speaker: MAYA
e09_n01.objective: Set the rescue target and immediate conflict.

events.text: It keeps sliding off the relay map.
Like it doesn't want to be found.
The pulse is right there on the display.
I heard Noah in the last transmission.
Nobody touches that hatch until we know what's moving inside it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That's easy for the dead engineer to say.
events.speaker: MAYA

effects.visibleConsequence: Maya trusts the operator's caution, but the clearer signal makes UNKNOWN easier to hear.
Maya remembers the operator rushed toward a moving pod before the beacon was stable.

events.text: Things that don't pretend to be dead friends.
Hale's filtering the beacon. I'm staying where the floor still agrees with me.
If I move, the beacon starts lying again.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: The relay pack is buzzing against my spine.

events.text: At least now it's lying in a straight line.
I hate that I can hear it better now.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: You bought us a cleaner line.
I can also hear the wrong thing better now.
Clip's fighting me. Give me a second.
Map's twitching. I'm moving before it lies again.
If this was reckless, remember you picked it.
Pod is closer than the map said.
Beacon dragged across my display while I moved.
Like the pod corrected where I thought it was.
Or the pod moved toward me.
Fast got us eyes on it.
Fast also let it see me back.

events.title: RESCUE ROUTE MAP
events.content:
ROUTE A: Short route through exposed radiation corridor. Arrival fast. Crew risk severe. Pod risk moderate.

ROUTE B: Long route through maintenance spine. Arrival delayed. Crew risk moderate. Pod risk severe.
events.caption: Recovered rescue route analysis. Both routes carry consequences.
events.message: [RESCUE ROUTE MAP ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Route A gets me there fast.
My suit takes the radiation. My body gets the invoice.
If you picked caution before, this is where it gets expensive.
If you like heroic stupidity, Route A is waving at you.
Pick the sin you can live with.
Corridor ahead. Radiation tags on both walls.
Warning lights are dead, because of course they are.
Do not stop in the exposed section.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: No talking. I need my legs to believe me.
Pretty sure I can taste metal.
Didn't know a place could do that.
If my hair falls out, I'm blaming you.
That's not a mood. That's a record.
Sensible is a nice word for slow when someone is suffocating.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Most of this is crawling. Lovely.
Don't expect charm when I get back.
Found older boot marks in the dust.
Someone used this route before us.
Both facts are sitting in my chest.
Hull is frosted from the inside.
Noah used to draw faces in condensation.

events.title: POD EXTERIOR SCAN
events.content:
Hull Temperature: below operational range
Seal Pressure: unstable
Interior Motion: intermittent
Audio Contamination: rising
Manual Override: possible
Warning: signal distortion resembles recursive voice fold
events.caption: Exterior scan of the pod. The pod is physically reachable but unstable.
events.message: [POD EXTERIOR SCAN ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

choices.text: SECURE THE POD BEFORE TOUCHING THE HATCH.

effects.mayaTrustVisible: Maya admits the operator's prep kept the pod from drifting.

effects.mayaResentmentVisible: Maya remembers the operator chose urgency and her shoulder paid for it.

events.text: If it moves, I move with it.
That is both reassuring and horrifying.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I wish that sentence felt less alive.
Like something inside answers the beacon.
Anchor makes the hatch less stupid.
If this goes badly, you picked urgency.
Whole frame is carrying motion through it.
I don't have to like it.
Before you touch that override, I need to say something.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I have seen this pod before.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That is such an Ethan answer.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: If you trusted me before, trust me now.
events.speaker: HALE

events.title: OLD POD ACCESS LOG
events.content:
Access Event: Emergency Pod Manual Inspection
Timestamp: Before Incident Alpha
Operator: Ethan Hale
Pod State: empty
Interior Residue: frost / no biological matter detected
Seal Reclosure: completed manually
Current Conflict: active motion and biometrics now detected inside same pod
events.caption: Recovered old access log. Hale previously opened the pod before Alpha.
events.message: [OLD POD ACCESS LOG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.title: HALE RECLOSURE NOTE
events.content:
ENGINEERING NOTE — ETHAN HALE
Manual reclosure completed.
Pod interior reported empty.
Frost pattern inconsistent with vacuum exposure.
Audio anomaly present after seal closure.
Personal note: I should have logged the voice.
events.caption: Hale's old pod reclosure note. It confirms the pod was closed again after an unexplained voice anomaly.
events.message: [HALE RECLOSURE NOTE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: I hate when a bad answer becomes documented.
And the system learned not to see it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Good. I needed something that wasn't just his voice.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Whatever is breathing in there is real enough for the pod to keep it alive.

event.speaker: HALE
event.text: Reality is exactly what this station edits best.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: Then keep talking while I work.

events.text: Looks old enough to hate me personally.
Hatch clicked twice while I worked.
That choice is standing right here with us.

events.title: POD ACCESS RISK REPORT
events.content:
Interior Motion: detected
Seal Pressure: unstable
Manual Override: required
Audio Channel Contamination: rising
External Voice Traffic: unauthorized
Recommendation: choose access protocol before opening
events.caption: Recovered access risk report. The pod can be opened, but the audio channel is contaminated.
events.message: [POD ACCESS RISK REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Fast means I may not control what comes out.
Valve wants to jump. I have to hold it.
It just means we know which damage we chose.
Something hit the inside of the hatch.
I heard it through my gloves.
I can feel it fighting the latch.
Hale is going to remember this.
Fast also woke the whole damn thing up.
you commit to whatever is inside.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: you may leave a person to die.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Whatever you choose, I carry it.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Keep your face away from the seam.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: it knows exactly which dead man to wear.
Or the pod is making him look like he is.
If that's Noah, we saved him.
If it isn't... we opened the door for it.
I hate you a little right now.

event.speaker: HALE
event.text: Maya, step away from the hatch.

events.text: Need to pull away before the beacon talks me back into this.
That feels like an answer we didn't ask for.
I can hear it clicking through the line.
If we survive the next room, I am going to be angry there too.
If Noah is in there, he heard us leave.
I need you to understand that.

directorCut.sourceVersion: THE RESCUE — ECHO LIFELINE PASS V2 + V7
majorChanges: CharacterBusy waits reduced from 180–240 seconds to 45–60 seconds.
Report-language lines rewritten into lived action/reaction language.
UNKNOWN messages rewritten to pressure story decisions instead of generic noise.
Route consequences strengthened: radiation exposure vs pod time loss.
Preparation consequences strengthened: anchored pod vs shoulder injury.
Hale proof branch strengthened with a new reclosure note artifact.
Vent/force branch now produces controlled-delay vs fast-contaminated states.
Open/abort branch now has stronger irreversible Episode 10 consequences.
Relationship consequence system rebuilt for Episode 10 usage.

episode10UsageNotes.beaconStabilized: Signal is clearer but UNKNOWN is easier to hear.
episode10UsageNotes.movedUnderPressure: Maya remembers the operator rushed her toward a moving pod.
episode10UsageNotes.mayaRadiationExposure: Maya should show physical strain and bitter humor in Episode 10.
episode10UsageNotes.podTimeLost: Noah or the pod occupant starts weaker because the safer route cost time.
episode10UsageNotes.podSecured: The pod is more stable at Episode 10 start.
episode10UsageNotes.mayaShoulderHit: Maya's shoulder pain should alter her typing/actions.
episode10UsageNotes.halePressed: Hale is cooperative but emotionally wounded because proof was demanded.
episode10UsageNotes.operatorPrioritizesRescueOverProof: Hale is more frustrated; Maya is more defiant.
episode10UsageNotes.openedPod: Episode 10 starts with the pod open and the occupant physically present/visible.
episode10UsageNotes.abortedRescue: Episode 10 starts with the pod closed and the beacon following the team.
episode10UsageNotes.stabilizedOpenPod: Safer open-pod start, but the occupant is weaker.
episode10UsageNotes.trackedBeaconAfterAbort: Abort route focuses on tracking the moving beacon.
episode10UsageNotes.reopenedRescueChannel: Abort route reopens comms and invites UNKNOWN pressure.
